examples.title: Measuring Your Height
examples.content: You're 170 cm tall. That's a length. The SI base unit for length is the meter. 170 cm = 1.7 meters. The meter (m) is one of the 7 SI base units—everyone worldwide agrees on it.

examples.title: Weighing Your Backpack
examples.content: Your backpack weighs 5 kg. That's a mass. The SI base unit for mass is the kilogram (kg). Everyone agrees: a kilogram is the mass of a specific cylinder in France (seriously!). So when you say 5 kg, everyone knows exactly what you mean.

examples.title: Timing a Race
examples.content: A 100-meter race takes 10 seconds to run. Time is a base unit: the second (s). Every scientist, every country, every person agrees on what a second is. No confusion!
